title: Document MeshCore app compatibility support matrix
description: Why this exists: users and future agents need a clear statement of what MeshCore app/client compatibility means for LICHEN. What needs to be done: document supported transport(s), identity/status/config fields, message send/receive flows, unsupported operations, semantic differences from MeshCore mesh behavior, known placeholders, and troubleshooting symptoms. Acceptance criteria: docs match implemented and smoke-tested behavior; unsupported features are explicit; RF non-compatibility is unambiguous; every remaining gap has a Bead.
notes: Implemented docs-only MeshCore compatibility support matrix in docs/meshcore-compat-dev.md. Matrix documents local BLE/NUS raw-frame compatibility only, no MeshCore RF interoperability, identity/device-info behavior, empty contacts, synthetic channel 0, read-only config placeholders, unsupported writes/admin/RF/private-key/signing flows, channel text ingress through app-interface, direct peer text current ERR_NOT_FOUND behavior, incoming event MSG_WAITING/SYNC_NEXT_MESSAGE behavior, raw-frame limits, troubleshooting symptoms, and remaining gap Beads. Created follow-up project-LICHEN-9c4y.21 for compatibility-local settings stores and project-LICHEN-9c4y.22 for direct peer prefix mapping after review found missing exact gap Beads and over-optimistic direct-peer wording. Existing gap Beads cited: project-LICHEN-9c4y.20 physical MeshCore BLE/client smoke, project-LICHEN-9c4y.8.2 physical incoming event validation, project-LICHEN-t2hn.7.2.2 gateway message-contract coverage. Checks: git diff --check PASS. No EC2 build required: docs/Beads-only change. Reviews: initial Russell found P2 gap-Bead/direct-peer wording issues, fixed with doc edits and new Beads; final Tesla no P0/P1/P2; final Carson no P0/P1/P2; final Harvey no P0/P1/P2 with only non-blocking note that device-time placeholder wording is loose but does not claim wall-clock support.
close_reason: Completed MeshCore compatibility support matrix. docs/meshcore-compat-dev.md now documents supported transport, identity/status/config surfaces, message ingress/egress, unsupported operations, RF non-compatibility, troubleshooting symptoms, and exact remaining gap Beads. Three final reviews found no P0/P1/P2.
